intro: Henningia afghanica, also known as Afghan Henningia, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Henningia afghanica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Henningia afghanica is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are considered valuable remedies in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever and respiratory infections.
